mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a robust and expanding economy. This leads to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 recovery has been characterized by steady job growth.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of instances where the 6-month change in PAYEMS is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, accommodative monetary policy lowers borrowing costs for firms and reduces the discount rate on future earnings, boosting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has maintained ZIRP since late 2008.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is not statistically different from the average return when FEDFUNDS > 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong real GDP growth and weakening housing starts indicate an imbalanced, late-cycle expansion. Weakness in the interest-rate sensitive housing sector acts as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown, predicting negative returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in GDPC1 > 2.5% AND year-over-year % change in HOUST < -5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a positive surprise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) increases the likelihood of more aggressive Fed rate hikes. This raises the equity risk premium and discount rates, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: During periods of rising FEDFUNDS, positive inflation surprises are not followed by negative 20-day returns on average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a contraction in the economy's productive capacity, signaling an impending economic slowdown and lower corporate profits, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of INDPRO month-over-month change turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO contractions are a classic recession indicator.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of cases where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates significant economic distress and slack in the labor market, depressing consumer sentiment and spending, which negatively impacts equity prices over a very short horizon as markets react to the news.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 6.0% AND rising for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment is a lagging but potent indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in retail sales (RSAFS) is a direct indicator of robust consumer spending, which constitutes the largest part of the US economy. This strength translates directly to higher corporate revenues and positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: A rolling 3-year rank correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a stagflationary environment. This is highly toxic for equities, as it implies eroding corporate margins, falling real demand, and a difficult policy trade-off for the central bank.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0.5% AND 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflationary period was a poor decade for equity returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero.
